Society 5.0 Digital Balance: Responsible Technology Use, and Addiction Prevention Support Program Evaluation Results Announced

10.06.2025

Three projects to be carried out on digital balance and technology addiction have been selected for support under the Society 5.0 Support Program.

Applications for the “Society 5.0 Digital Balance: Responsible Technology Use, and Addiction Prevention Support Program,” launched by our university to support research that understands the societal impacts of the digital age and aims to transform these impacts into social benefit, have been evaluated.

Evaluations were conducted based on four main criteria: project objectives and goals (20 points), originality (30 points), methodology (20 points), and widespread impact (30 points). As a result, three projects have been selected for support.

Among the applications submitted under the 2025 call, the projects that successfully passed the evaluation process and qualified for support are as follows:

Projects Selected for Support:

From the Virtual World to the Real World: Digital Shadows – Mehmet IŞIK

Istanbul Cyber Security Research – Aylin Tutgun ÜNAL

Simav Digital Addiction Prevention Workshop – Esra KESİM

We congratulate the project leaders who have qualified for support and wish them continued success.

For more information and to submit your questions regarding the process, please contact us at pdo@ihu.edu.tr .

Note:

Orientation and contract-related information for the supported projects will be provided separately.
